items.title: VinFast Bets on Entrepreneurs with “Rentapasada” Program in the Philippines
items.summary:
Most automakers entering a new market concentrate on selling vehicles. VinFast is taking a different approach in the Philippines, where it is trying to create new business owners first. The Vietnamese electric vehicle manufacturer has expanded its Rentapasada (literally rent-to-hire) program, an initiative that allows Filipinos to launch a ride-hailing   ... [continued]
The post VinFast Bets on Entrepreneurs with “Rentapasada” Program in the Philippines appeared first on CleanTechnica.

items.title: New 6 passenger, 780-mile PHEV from GM has North America written all over it
items.summary: GM thinks its newest Starlight L PHEV SUV could be China’s best value hybrid, offering 260 km (~160 miles) of all-electric range, spacious six-passenger seating, and a modern technology package at an affordable price. And it’s exactly the kind of vehicle that the company could try to rebadge for North America
items.translatedTitle: 来自通用汽车的新6名乘客， 780英里的PHEV在北美到处写满

items.title: LiuGong brings electric heavy equipment options to North America [video]
items.summary: This week, Chinese equipment brand LiuGong officially launched its battery-electric equipment and material handling asset portfolio in the United States and Canada, making it easier than ever for construction and forestry crews to decarbonize their fleets.
items.translatedTitle: 柳工将电动重型设备带到北美[视频]

items.title: Honda goes all-in on EVs in a multibillion dollar market: commercial lawnmowers
items.summary: Honda Automobile dealers will have to wait a little longer for a self-driving EV that seriously worry Tesla or GM – but Honda Power Equipment dealers are playing in a multibillion market, too, and they have an advanced, autonomous, and all-electric vehicle arriving right now.
items.translatedTitle: 在价值数十亿美元的市场中，本田全力以赴开发电动汽车：商用割草机

items.summary:
Trump or no Trump, the global offshore wind industry is taking advantage of new cost-cutting technologies and a new generation of offshore construction vessels that can handle today's supersized turbines.

items.summary: TrashBench recently decided to test whether adding more and more fans to a powerful GPU would improve its performance.

items.title: Philippines Logistics Firm Sees Swappable Battery Scooters As The Next Milestone In Last-Mile Deliveries
items.summary:
Philippines’ express delivery service JRS Express and mobility startup Voltai finalized a pilot partnership to integrate electric motorcycles and battery-swapping technology into commercial logistics operations in Metro Manila last March. As of July 16, the partnership has begun running a real-world testing ground for two-wheel electric vehicles (EVs) operating within   ... [continued]

items.title: New Delhi Air Pollution Index Further Pushes for EV Adoption in India
items.summary:
India’s latest push toward electric vehicles is being driven by more than industrial policy or climate commitments. It is also a response to one of the country’s most persistent public‑health crises: air pollution. The Delhi government has approved a sweeping new electric‑vehicle policy that offers owners of older vehicles more   ... [continued]

items.summary:
The Department of Science and Technology (DOST) is mounting a coordinated push to scale up homegrown electric vehicle technologies as volatile global fuel prices continue to pressure the local transport sector. Driven by its central research arm, the Philippine Council for Industry, Energy, and Emerging Technology Research and Development, the   ... [continued]

items.title: Radar EV Pickup Sales More Than Double in Philippines as Electric Trucks Gain Traction
items.summary:
UAAGI Auto Group, the Philippine distributor of the battery-electric Radar RD6 pickup, said it sold 112 units in June, more than doubling the 44 units delivered in May. While the company did not disclose cumulative sales, the month-over-month increase suggests growing interest in electrified pickups, a segment that has recently   ... [continued]
The post Radar EV Pickup Sales More Than Double in Philippines as Electric Trucks Gain Traction appeared first on CleanTechnica.

items.title: Nvidia RTX 50 Super GPUs are reportedly ready, but stuck in limbo due to excessive GDDR7 pricing — 3GB GDDR7 module costs triple the price of 2GB
items.summary: The 3GB GDDR7 chips that the RTX 50 Super GPUs will use reportedly cost twice to thrice as much as the 2GB chips found on vanilla RTX 50-series graphics cards.  This would likely push the retail price of these GPUs way beyond Nvidia's target MSRP.
items.translatedTitle: 据报道， Nvidia RTX 50 Super GPU已准备就绪，但由于GDDR7定价过高而陷入困境— 3GB GDDR7模块的价格是2GB价格的三倍

items.title: Philippines Energy Company Junk Coal Plant & Break Ground on Latest BESS
items.summary:
Coal is dying a slow death in the central Philippines, and giant batteries are moving into the neighborhood to take its place. The Visayas power grid has suffered from chronic reliability issues for years, leaving the province of Cebu vulnerable to rolling blackouts during peak periods. As regional authorities push   ... [continued]
The post Philippines Energy Company Junk Coal Plant & Break Ground on Latest BESS appeared first on CleanTechnica.
items.translatedTitle: 菲律宾能源公司垃圾煤电厂在最新的BESS上破土动工

items.title: New HMP Flash brings affordable 75 MPH electric scooters to the US
items.summary: Electric scooters are everywhere in much of Europe and Asia, but they’ve always remained something of an oddity in the US market. While American riders have recently embraced electric bicycles and, to a measurably smaller extent, electric motorcycles, traditional seated scooters have struggled to gain traction in North America. Now San Francisco-based HMP Bikes is hoping to change that with its new HMP Flash, a high-performance electric scooter designed from the ground up for riders who rack up serious mileage.
items.translatedTitle: 全新HMP Flash为美国带来经济实惠的75英里/小时电动滑板车

items.title: AGI AI828 SSD Review: A near-last resort for those on a budget
items.summary: The AGI AI828 is a budget drive with subpar performance and power efficiency. This makes it a last resort, although in the current market, it might be good enough for some.
items.translatedTitle: AGI AI828 SSD Review ：预算有限的人士的最后度假胜地

items.title: APsystems introduces plug-in microinverter for U.S. balcony solar
items.summary:

The post APsystems introduces plug-in microinverter for U.S. balcony solar appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: APsystems为美国阳台太阳能引入插电式微型逆变器

items.title: How to protect residential batteries from heat waves
items.summary:
An expert from Kiwa PI Berlin recommends installing batteries in thermally stable locations, ensuring proper ventilation, following manufacturer guidelines, and using certified equipment to reduce heat-related risks. During heatwaves, shifting daytime electricity consumption to direct solar use can help limit battery stress and prevent overheating.
The post How to protect residential batteries from heat waves appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: 如何保护住宅电池免受热浪影响

items.title: ‘Inclusion has to move beyond good intentions and symbolic actions’
items.summary:
This week Women in Solar+ Europe gives voice to Floriane de Brabandère. Founding President of the French National Solar Energy Federation (FNES). She says Inclusion is also about providing people with the practical means to succeed. "You cannot retain talent that is poorly equipped," she states. 
The post ‘Inclusion has to move beyond good intentions and symbolic actions’ appeared first on pv magazine Global.
